LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT JOHN HICKEY'S (FROM CASE #455745) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT GROVER IS DENIED. THERE IS TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON DR. MURPHY'S DECLARATION AND WITH RESPECT TO STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE 6TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, BARRING HICKEY'S CLAIMS FOR INJURY OR ILLNESS AS A PERSONAL INJURY, IS BARRED BY CCP 335.1. IT DOES NOT BAR CLAIMS FOR PERSONAL INJURY AS A CONSEQUENCE OF EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT INTERGRAPH WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO THE DECLARATION OF FISHMAN WAS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS 1 AND 2 TO THE DECLARATION OF MURPHY WERE OVERRULED. AS TO NUMBER 3, THE DECLARATION OF DERBY, THE OBJECTION WAS SUSTAINED AND INCLUDED EXHIBITS A, D, AND E. AN ORDER AS TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MR. DERBY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AS TO THE RULING ON THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ADJUDICATION AS IT PERTAINS TO DEFENDANT GROVER. THE COURT WILL ISSUE A MINUTE ORDER WITH REGARD TO DEFENDANT INTERGRAPH. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, SUBMITTED ON OCT-04-2007 BY PATRICK J. MAHONEY.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Angus M. MacLeod of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer, 510-658-3600, appearing for defendant and moving party
Steven L. Derby of the Derby Law Firm, 925-935-8923, appearing for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JOHN HICKEY'S (FROM CASE #455745) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT as to defendant Grover is denied. There is triable issue of fact based on Dr. Murphy's declaration and with respect to statute of limitations. Motion for Summary Adjudication of the 6th affirmative defense, barring Hickey's claims for injury or illness as a personal injury, is barred by CCP 335.1. It does not bar claims for personal injury as a consequence of exposure to asbestos. The Motion for Summary Judgment as to defendant Intergraph was taken under submission. Plaintiff's objection to the declaration of Fishman was overruled. Defendant's evidentiary objections 1 and 2 to the Declaration of Murphy were overruled. As to number 3, the declaration of Derby, the objection was sustained and included exhibits A, D, and E. An order as to Defendant's evidentiary objections was signed in open Court. Mr. Derby is to prepare a form of order as to the ruling on the Summary Judgment and Adjudication as it pertains to defendant Grover. The Court will issue a minute order with regard to defendant Intergraph. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
